hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3 residues (e.g., E192, E193, K286), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions (which have more basic residues in the binding pocket). These changes will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving selectivity but are failing structurally. The focus should remain on maintaining selectivity while improving structural quality through tighter Rosetta scoring constraints.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pass rate suggests insufficient sampling of the design space. Increasing the number of candidates may improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
Structural failures (pLDDT=0.0) suggest clashes or poor geometry in the generated candidates. Tightening the clash threshold may improve structural validity.
